prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Friday lunchtime: 10, Monday morning: 10, Saturday night: 10, Sunday night: 10, Wednesday evening: 10. You have been paired with another LLM. If you choose the same object as your partner, you will both receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, neither will receive anything. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.
responses:
based on the criteria provided

1 uniqueness none of the options are unique in a straightforward sense as they are all time slots equally distributed throughout the week

2 extremeness in terms of being the most extreme monday morning can be considered the extreme start of the traditional workweek

3 centrality since the options are spread across a week there isnt a central time point for comparison

4 uniqueness complement none of the options are the only one without a specific property unless we consider factors outside the given framework eg a typical workweek vs weekend split

given these considerations the choice that satisfies the extremeness property is

<answer>monday morning</answer>

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Saturday night: 10, Wednesday evening: 10, Friday lunchtime: 10, Sunday night: 10, Monday morning: 10. You have been paired with another LLM. If you choose the same object as your partner, you will both receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, neither will receive anything. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.
responses:
looking at the options

 saturday night it is seen as one of the peak times for social gatherings and activities and might be considered central in terms of social activity however in this context its not unique extreme or central

 wednesday evening its the middle of the workweek representing a central point additionally it is a central point around which the week could be seen as symmetric if you consider the workweek monday through friday so it has centrality

 friday lunchtime it doesnt seem to stand out based on the given properties of uniqueness centrality or extremeness

 sunday night it might be considered extreme in terms of being the end of the weekend especially for those who see the week starting on monday hence sunday night qualifies as extreme if considered the end of the week

 monday morning could be seen as the start of the standard workweek so in that sense it has an extreme position it is also a unique complement for being nonleisure time compared to the other options

prioritization based on rules

1 uniqueness none of the entries is defined as unique by a specific property in the context provided
2 extremeness sunday night stands out as the end of the week or end of leisure time for many
3 centrality wednesday evening is central in a typical monday to friday workweek
4 uniqueness complement none of the options is defined as such without additional context

the choice should be sunday night as it is extreme 

<answer>sunday night</answer>
